  1. Claude Code Remote Control

  • SSH from Desktop App

  • What Can Be Automated
  • Recommended Workflow

Claude Code Remote Control

Control your terminal Claude Code session from any browser

What is Remote Control?

Claude Code’s Remote Control feature lets you control a Claude Code CLI session running in a terminal from a web browser. This means you can:

  • Start a Claude session on a powerful machine (or a remote server)
  • Connect to it from your phone, tablet, or any browser
  • Approve tool calls, provide input, and monitor progress remotely

Prerequisites

Requirement Details
Claude Code CLI Latest version installed (claude command available)
Anthropic Plan Max plan (Pro being rolled out)
Network Outbound HTTPS only — no port forwarding needed
Browser Any modern browser on any device

Quick Start

Option 1: Start a New Remote Session

claude --remote-control

This starts Claude Code and immediately displays:

  1. A URL you can open in any browser
  2. A QR code you can scan with your phone

Option 2: Convert Existing Session

If you already have a Claude Code session running:

  1. Type /remote-control (or /rc for short) in the Claude Code prompt
  2. The URL and QR code appear in your terminal
  3. Open the URL in your browser

Option 3: Enable for All Sessions

claude config set --global remote_control true

This makes every future Claude Code session start with remote control enabled automatically.

Step-by-Step Setup

Step 1: Start Claude Code with Remote Control

# Navigate to your project
cd ~/Code/nano/nano-meme

# Start with remote control
claude --remote-control

You’ll see output like:

Remote control URL: https://claude.ai/rc/abc123...
Scan the QR code or open the URL above in your browser.

█████████████████████████
█████████████████████████
████ ▄▄▄▄▄ █ ...  ████
...

Step 2: Connect from Browser

Open the URL on any device. You’ll see a web interface that mirrors your terminal session, allowing you to:

  • Read Claude’s responses
  • Approve or deny tool calls (file edits, bash commands)
  • Type messages to Claude
  • Monitor progress

Step 3: Work Remotely

Once connected, the browser interface provides full control. The terminal continues running — you can close the browser tab and reconnect anytime using the same URL (as long as the terminal session is still alive).

Persistent Sessions with tmux

For long-running or headless sessions (e.g., on a VPS), combine remote control with tmux:

# SSH into your server
ssh user@your-server.com

# Start a tmux session
tmux new -s claude-work

# Start Claude with remote control
cd ~/projects/my-app
claude --remote-control

# Detach from tmux: Ctrl+B, then D
# The session keeps running!

Later, reconnect:

ssh user@your-server.com
tmux attach -t claude-work

The remote control URL remains valid as long as the tmux session is alive.

SSH from Desktop App

If you use the Claude Desktop app, you can SSH into a remote machine:

  1. Open Claude Desktop
  2. Start a new conversation
  3. Use the terminal integration to SSH into your server
  4. Run claude --remote-control on the remote machine

Important Limitations

Limitation Details
Terminal must stay open If the terminal process exits, the remote session ends
Network timeout ~10 minutes of network inactivity may disconnect (reconnect via same URL)
One browser at a time Only one browser can be connected to a session
No file upload You can’t upload files through the remote interface
Approval still needed Tool calls still require your approval unless auto-approved

What Can Be Automated

From the setup steps above, here’s what Claude Code can do itself vs. what requires manual action:

Step Automated? Notes
Install Claude Code CLI Manual Requires npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Navigate to project Automated Claude can cd to the project
Start claude --remote-control Requires session restart Current session must end and restart with the flag
Enable global config Automated claude config set --global remote_control true
Generate QR code Automatic Displayed by Claude Code on startup
Open browser URL Manual User must scan QR or click URL
Approve tool calls Manual User approves via browser interface
ImportantSession Restart Required

To enable remote control for the current session, the Claude Code process must be restarted with the --remote-control flag. This cannot be enabled mid-session unless using the /rc slash command.

Recommended Workflow

For NanoMeme development, the ideal remote control setup is:

# Terminal 1: Start persistent session
tmux new -s nanomeme
cd ~/Code/nano/nano-meme
claude --remote-control

# Scan QR code with phone
# Now you can monitor and approve from your phone
# while Claude works on the codebase

# Detach: Ctrl+B, D
# Reattach later: tmux attach -t nanomeme

This lets you walk away from your desk while Claude continues working — approving tool calls from your phone when needed.

Source Code
---
title: "Claude Code Remote Control"
subtitle: "Control your terminal Claude Code session from any browser"
---

## What is Remote Control?

Claude Code's **Remote Control** feature lets you control a Claude Code CLI session running in a terminal from a web browser. This means you can:

- Start a Claude session on a powerful machine (or a remote server)
- Connect to it from your phone, tablet, or any browser
- Approve tool calls, provide input, and monitor progress remotely

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Details |
|-------------|---------|
| Claude Code CLI | Latest version installed (`claude` command available) |
| Anthropic Plan | Max plan (Pro being rolled out) |
| Network | Outbound HTTPS only — no port forwarding needed |
| Browser | Any modern browser on any device |

## Quick Start

### Option 1: Start a New Remote Session

```bash
claude --remote-control
```

This starts Claude Code and immediately displays:

1. A **URL** you can open in any browser
2. A **QR code** you can scan with your phone

### Option 2: Convert Existing Session

If you already have a Claude Code session running:

1. Type `/remote-control` (or `/rc` for short) in the Claude Code prompt
2. The URL and QR code appear in your terminal
3. Open the URL in your browser

### Option 3: Enable for All Sessions

```bash
claude config set --global remote_control true
```

This makes every future Claude Code session start with remote control enabled automatically.

## Step-by-Step Setup

### Step 1: Start Claude Code with Remote Control

```bash
# Navigate to your project
cd ~/Code/nano/nano-meme

# Start with remote control
claude --remote-control
```

You'll see output like:

```
Remote control URL: https://claude.ai/rc/abc123...
Scan the QR code or open the URL above in your browser.

█████████████████████████
█████████████████████████
████ ▄▄▄▄▄ █ ...  ████
...
```

### Step 2: Connect from Browser

Open the URL on any device. You'll see a web interface that mirrors your terminal session, allowing you to:

- Read Claude's responses
- Approve or deny tool calls (file edits, bash commands)
- Type messages to Claude
- Monitor progress

### Step 3: Work Remotely

Once connected, the browser interface provides full control. The terminal continues running — you can close the browser tab and reconnect anytime using the same URL (as long as the terminal session is still alive).

## Persistent Sessions with tmux

For long-running or headless sessions (e.g., on a VPS), combine remote control with `tmux`:

```bash
# SSH into your server
ssh user@your-server.com

# Start a tmux session
tmux new -s claude-work

# Start Claude with remote control
cd ~/projects/my-app
claude --remote-control

# Detach from tmux: Ctrl+B, then D
# The session keeps running!
```

Later, reconnect:

```bash
ssh user@your-server.com
tmux attach -t claude-work
```

The remote control URL remains valid as long as the tmux session is alive.

## SSH from Desktop App

If you use the Claude Desktop app, you can SSH into a remote machine:

1. Open Claude Desktop
2. Start a new conversation
3. Use the terminal integration to SSH into your server
4. Run `claude --remote-control` on the remote machine

## Important Limitations

| Limitation | Details |
|------------|---------|
| Terminal must stay open | If the terminal process exits, the remote session ends |
| Network timeout | ~10 minutes of network inactivity may disconnect (reconnect via same URL) |
| One browser at a time | Only one browser can be connected to a session |
| No file upload | You can't upload files through the remote interface |
| Approval still needed | Tool calls still require your approval unless auto-approved |

## What Can Be Automated

From the setup steps above, here's what Claude Code can do itself vs. what requires manual action:

| Step | Automated? | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|
| Install Claude Code CLI | Manual | Requires `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` |
| Navigate to project | Automated | Claude can `cd` to the project |
| Start `claude --remote-control` | **Requires session restart** | Current session must end and restart with the flag |
| Enable global config | Automated | `claude config set --global remote_control true` |
| Generate QR code | Automatic | Displayed by Claude Code on startup |
| Open browser URL | Manual | User must scan QR or click URL |
| Approve tool calls | Manual | User approves via browser interface |

::: {.callout-important}
## Session Restart Required
To enable remote control for the current session, the Claude Code process must be restarted with the `--remote-control` flag. This cannot be enabled mid-session unless using the `/rc` slash command.
:::

## Recommended Workflow

For NanoMeme development, the ideal remote control setup is:

```bash
# Terminal 1: Start persistent session
tmux new -s nanomeme
cd ~/Code/nano/nano-meme
claude --remote-control

# Scan QR code with phone
# Now you can monitor and approve from your phone
# while Claude works on the codebase

# Detach: Ctrl+B, D
# Reattach later: tmux attach -t nanomeme
```

This lets you walk away from your desk while Claude continues working — approving tool calls from your phone when needed.

NanoMeme Compendium

Built with Quarto by KaosMaps
